eventMetadata.context_description: Malta enters the next T20I in their home 6-match series against Gibraltar with momentum from a commanding 50-run victory in the opener at Marsa Sports Club, where they posted 231/4 before restricting Gibraltar to 181/7. This result reinforces Malta's 6-2 head-to-head edge in T20Is against these associate nations, bolstered by superior recent form including multiple wins over similar opponents. Gibraltar must improve their bowling discipline and chase execution on a batting-friendly pitch, while Malta's strong top-order batting—led by contributions from Varun Thamotharam and Waseem Abbas—provides a clear advantage. Home conditions, short turnaround between fixtures, and no reported injuries shape trader consensus toward Malta's favored status in this closely packed series.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CA-35 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-35 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Norma Torres holds a commanding lead in California's 35th Congressional District, rated Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report with a D+6 partisan voting index, driving trader consensus to price Democrats at 93.5% on Polymarket. Recent campaign finance reports through March 31 reveal Torres's $381,000 cash-on-hand dwarfing Republican Mike Cargile's $2,550, echoing her double-digit victories over him in 2020, 2022, and 2024 despite the district's rightward swing among Latino voters. The June 2 top-two primary likely advances Torres against Cargile or another Democrat, cementing her path. Scenarios like a Torres scandal, withdrawal, or national Republican wave exceeding historical midterm shifts could challenge this, though barriers remain high.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Ittihad sit sixth in the Saudi Pro League table with 42 points from 26 matches, but their recent form has faltered with just one win in the last five games (1W-2D-2L), exacerbated by the winter departures of stars Karim Benzema to Al Hilal and N'Golo Kanté, leaving gaps in attack and midfield amid fan frustration ahead of a King's Cup semi-final. Al Shabab, 12th on 29 points, host at SHG Arena on April 10 showing sharper scoring edge lately (three wins in five, 12 goals), buoyed by home strength despite mid-table position and forward Mohammed Harbush sidelined long-term with a cruciate injury. Al Ittihad hold a strong head-to-head edge, unbeaten in the last five meetings, setting up a competitive Riyadh clash influenced by rest advantages and tactical adjustments.

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Indianapolis Colts in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku remains unsigned as the top remaining veteran option post-2026 NFL Draft, driving tightly bunched trader consensus around 45-47% implied probabilities for TE-needy contenders like the Bengals, Cardinals, Broncos, Chargers, and Dolphins, all flush with cap space and quarterback weapons to pair with his 4,000-yard career production. His February farewell to the Browns—citing a desire for new scenery amid injury concerns that sidelined him for 11 games over two seasons—dropped Cleveland to 24.5%, while recent visits to Miami (April 10) and a Monday Chargers meeting fueled speculation without a deal. Falcons and Ravens links add depth, underscoring competitive free agency dynamics with no clear frontrunner as minicamp nears.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 9°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 41% implied probability for London's lowest temperature on May 9 reaching 14°C or higher, driven by Met Office seasonal outlooks signaling near- or above-average spring temperatures in southeast England amid an emerging El Niño pattern (61% chance of onset by May-July per NOAA). This favors suppressed nocturnal cooling under anticipated settled high-pressure influences, amplified by London's urban heat island effect, which typically elevates city minima 1-3°C above rural Heathrow averages of ~9°C. Equal 25.5% odds across 6-13°C outcomes reflect model uncertainty over cloud cover and light winds, which could enable radiative cooling, while sub-5°C options trail due to historical rarity absent cold outbreaks. Met Office daily forecast updates on May 8 will likely sharpen these probabilities.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 46.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Yellow Submarine and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 8 at 5:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 47 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Yellow Submarine vs REKONIX (BO3) - 1win Essence Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Yellow Submarine and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 8 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Yellow Submarine" if Yellow Submarine win the match against REKONIX.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against Yellow Submarine.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yellow Submarine enters the 1win Essence I playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal as undefeated Group A winners with a perfect 4-0 series record and 8-3 map score, fueled by recent 2-0 shutout over 1w Team on May 6 and 2-1 triumphs against Nemiga Gaming, MOUZ, and PlayTime. REKONIX clinched Group B's second seed at 2-2 (5-5 maps), buoyed by a 2-1 upset of Team Nemesis on May 5 but hampered by losses to Nigma Galaxy and Zero Tenacity. In this double-elimination BO3 clash, trader consensus reflects Yellow Submarine's tournament momentum and CIS synergy versus REKONIX's SEA qualifier grit, with no head-to-head history and draft adaptations to patch 7.41 meta pivotal amid stable rosters.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Will the Eurozone's 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.6% and 1.8%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone headline HICP inflation surged to 3.0% in April 2026—its highest since late 2023—driven by a 10.9% spike in energy prices amid Middle East tensions, including the Iran conflict, propelling Polymarket traders to price a 44.4% implied probability for December 2026 year-on-year HICP exceeding 3.1%. This positioning reflects ECB staff's March upward revision to 2.6% average inflation for 2026, confirmed by the May 4 Survey of Professional Forecasters at 2.7% with core at 2.2%, amid sticky services pressures and risks of energy pass-through despite meager 0.1% Q1 GDP growth. Hawkish ECB comments signal potential rate hikes, curbing easing expectations; final April data due mid-May and June FOMC projections loom as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

markets.question: AD Ceuta FC vs. CD Castellón: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game between AD Ceuta FC and CD Castellón, scheduled for May 9 at 8:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both AD Ceuta FC and CD Castellón each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AD Ceuta FC vs. CD Castellón - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 9 at 8:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: CD Castellón hold fourth in LaLiga 2 standings with promotion playoff aspirations, bolstered by a recent 3-1 home win over Burgos and solid away form (5 wins in 18), while AD Ceuta FC sit 12th, pushing for safety after a crucial 2-2 draw at Zaragoza that reached the 50-point mark. The return fixture follows their earlier 3-3 stalemate, highlighting Ceuta's home resilience at Alfonso Murube despite ongoing defensive frailties conceding regularly. Key absences include Ceuta's Rubén Díez (muscle injury, late April doubt) and Castellón's Douglas Aurélio (meniscus); disciplinary risks loom for Castellón's Rubén Catalá and others. Late-season table pressure and travel fatigue could sway this competitive Segunda División clash.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 19°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.title: Highest temperature in Madrid on May 8?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 80% implied probability to a highest temperature of 18°C or below at Adolfo Suárez Madrid–Barajas Airport on May 8, driven by AEMET's latest observations and ECMWF/GFS ensemble forecasts clustering maxima at 17–18°C amid persistent overcast skies and scattered showers. A low-pressure system over the Iberian Peninsula has advected cool Atlantic air masses via northerly flows, suppressing daytime insolation and convective heating—evident in early-morning readings below 12°C at Barajas and Retiro stations. Yesterday's Retiro maximum of 18.3°C aligns with this below-normal pattern (May climatological average ~23°C), while 80% precipitation odds and potential thunderstorms further cap warming potential. Updated AEMET bulletins later today could refine hourly trajectories as the resolution window closes.

markets.question: Will Clavicular be named People's Sexiest Man Alive in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the name of the person who is named People Magazine's Sexiest Man Alive.

If more than one person is named Sexiest Man Alive by People Magazine, this market will resolve in favor of the person whose name comes first in alphabetical order.

If no Sexiest Man Alive is announced for 2026 by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other". 

The resolution source for this market will be People (https://people.com/) or a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Caroline Elliott at 66% implied probability to win the Conservative Party of British Columbia leadership, propelled by the latest Pallas Data poll of 1,253 party members showing her 31% first-preference support—well ahead of Kerry-Lynne Findlay (24%) and Iain Black (18%)—amid 37% ballot exhaustion rates that shrink the electorate in subsequent preferential ballot rounds, benefiting the frontrunner. Voting opens May 23 post-verification, with results May 30.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Vasylivka by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Vasylivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.357760° N, 37.038017° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces approached the outskirts of Vasylivka in Donetsk Oblast via advances along the Hryshyne and Novooleksandrivka axes in late April, prompting intense frontline clashes, but Ukrainian troops have since rolled back those gains through infiltrations south of Prymorske and pressure on the eastern Russian salient as of early May. No verified Russian control or infiltration of Vasylivka proper appears on ISW maps, DeepStateMap, or geolocated footage, with Ukrainian General Staff reporting repelled attacks amid daily mechanized assaults and drone strikes. Trader consensus reflects the closely contested dynamics, hinging on potential escalation before the May 31 resolution deadline tied to persistent ISW shading; overall Russian net territorial losses in April underscore Ukrainian defensive resilience in this Pokrovsk-direction push.

markets.question: Game Handicap: GEN (-1.5) vs Kiwoom DRX (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Kiwoom DRX and Gen.G in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 8 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G" if Gen.G wins 2 or more games than Kiwoom DRX in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.
markets.groupItemTitle: Game Handicap: GEN (-1.5) vs Kiwoom DRX (+1.5)

events.title: LoL: Kiwoom DRX vs Gen.G (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Kiwoom DRX and Gen.G in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 8 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Kiwoom DRX" if Kiwoom DRX win the match against Gen.G.

This market will resolve to "Gen.G" if Gen.G win the match against Kiwoom DRX.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gen.G enters this LCK Rounds 1-2 BO3 series as the stronger contender against Kiwoom DRX following their 2-1 victory over the same opponent in Week 5 Day 2, driven by Kiin's dominant top lane and Canyon’s jungle pressure. Kiwoom DRX responded with a decisive 2-0 sweep of Nongshim RedForce six days ago, showcasing improved macro play and sponge's aggressive Lee Sin picks amid their post-offseason roster rebuild. Gen.G sits higher in standings with consistent form from stars like Chovy and Ruler, while DRX leverages recent momentum and head-to-head resilience; no major injuries or patch shifts alter the closely contested matchup dynamics.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's confidential SEC filing for a mid-2026 initial public offering, targeting a $1.75–2 trillion valuation with a potential June roadshow and Nasdaq listing, drives the strong 60.5% implied probability on the 2.0T+ outcome, reflecting trader consensus on explosive growth from Starship reusable launch vehicle advancements and Starlink satellite constellation expansion. Recent Starship V4 thrust upgrades and frequent test flights underscore operational momentum, elevating private share prices to $633 amid secondary tenders. Governance scrutiny from unions and investors over Elon Musk's sweeping powers introduces minor downside risk to lower brackets like 1.8T–2.0T (13.5%), but no pre-2028 IPO odds remain low at 2.8% ahead of key regulatory milestones.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's closely contested March 22 parliamentary election, which produced a hung National Assembly with no outright majority, incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement conceded on April 20 that it could not secure a coalition, paving the way for Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party-led right-wing bloc to lead government formation talks. Recent advances, including parliament's April 29 approval of a trimmed Government Act and Janša's May 5 announcement of agreed coalition guidelines, have solidified trader consensus around his commanding position, reflecting the bloc's combined seats and momentum toward a majority. While a parliamentary confidence vote remains pending, potential challenges include stalled negotiations over policy sticking points, a presidential mandate to another leader like Jernej Vrtovec, or a snap election if no government forms within constitutional timelines.

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 120-139 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Democrats at 60.5% to win the 2028 US presidential election, up from recent lows, amid President Trump's term limits creating an open-seat contest and heavy institutional betting volumes surpassing $1 billion on related nominee markets. This surge to all-time highs in late April reflects optimism in the Democratic field, with California Governor Gavin Newsom leading nominee odds at around 26%, while Vice President JD Vance holds Republican frontrunner status near 38%. The electoral map mirrors 2024's battlegrounds—Pennsylvania pivotal, with swing states like Wisconsin and Michigan tougher for Republicans absent Trump's ballot presence—compounded by markets pricing Democratic gains in the 2026 midterms (87% House control odds). Structural factors from the 2020 census provide Democrats a slight edge, though candidate quality and economic conditions remain key wildcards ahead of primaries.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 41% implied probability for London's lowest temperature on May 9 reaching 14°C or higher, driven by Met Office seasonal outlooks signaling near- or above-average spring temperatures in southeast England amid an emerging El Niño pattern (61% chance of onset by May-July per NOAA). This favors suppressed nocturnal cooling under anticipated settled high-pressure influences, amplified by London's urban heat island effect, which typically elevates city minima 1-3°C above rural Heathrow averages of ~9°C. Equal 25.5% odds across 6-13°C outcomes reflect model uncertainty over cloud cover and light winds, which could enable radiative cooling, while sub-5°C options trail due to historical rarity absent cold outbreaks. Met Office daily forecast updates on May 8 will likely sharpen these probabilities.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: ONIC vs Natus Vincere (BO3) - MPL Indonesia Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between ONIC and Natus Vincere in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 9 at 9:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ONIC" if ONIC win the match against Natus Vincere.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against ONIC.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will fewer than 25 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between April 27-May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from April 27, 2026, through May 3, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 98.6% implied probability for 25-49 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during the week of April 27, 2026, driven by drastically reduced maritime traffic amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict and near-blockade conditions. Real-time AIS data from MarineTraffic and shipping trackers like Windward report daily crossings averaging 5-11 vessels—mainly dry bulk and occasional LNG carriers—versus the pre-crisis norm of 100-138, with April's total at just 191 ships per CNN analysis. Heightened risks from IRGC seizures, uninsurable premiums, and sporadic firings have deterred commercial operators, anchoring over 1,000 vessels outside the strait. While IMF Portwatch data will finalize resolution, a realistic challenge would require upward revisions from underreported transits or an improbable de-escalation surge before May 3 cutoff.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

eventMetadata.context_description: Somaliland's House of Elders approved a 27-month term extension for the House of Representatives on April 28, pushing parliamentary elections well beyond 2027 and driving trader consensus to 64.5% on no polls before then, amid repeated delays from an original May 2026 schedule. The National Electoral Commission had recommended a shorter extension for voter preparations, but the Guurti overrode it, sparking protests from opposition candidates decrying unconstitutional overreach. Waddani tops party odds at 32.6% on momentum from President Abdirahman Irro's 2024 presidential win under their banner, while former ruling Kulmiye trails at 0.3% post-defeat and UCID holds 4.0% as a perennial minor contender. Legal challenges and political pressures could still influence timelines.

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Hart Memorial Trophy, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid leads trader consensus for the Hart Memorial Trophy at 46.4% implied probability after clinching the Art Ross Trophy with a league-high 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists) in 82 games, powering the Oilers with a point in all 41 wins and finishing eight ahead of Nikita Kucherov’s 130 points in 76 games. Kucherov, at 31.3%, surged late despite missing games, joining McDavid as Ted Lindsay Award finalists alongside rookie Macklin Celebrini, while Nathan MacKinnon’s 16.0% reflects his Maurice "Rocket" Richard win (53 goals) and dominant 5-on-5 metrics (+58 goals for-against differential). Playoff first-round exits for Edmonton and Tampa have not shifted regular-season-focused voting, emphasizing the trio's elite production amid tight standings races.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Tokyo be 14°C on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects profound uncertainty for Tokyo's lowest temperature on May 9, with all outcomes from 13°C or below to 23°C or higher priced equally at 40.5% implied probability, signaling low liquidity and divergent short-range forecast models. The Japan Meteorological Agency's latest guidance, issued May 6, projects a daily low of 14°C amid mild southerly flows and partial cloudiness, following early May's cooler anomalies around 12°C that resolved prior markets lower. Differentiating factors include ensemble model spreads capturing variable nocturnal radiative cooling under potential clear skies versus cloud-induced warmth, frontal passage timing, and Tokyo's urban heat island boosting minima at the official observatory; JMA's May 8 update could sharpen odds as resolution nears.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Darth Vader" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's verbal criticism of Pope Leo XIV on May 5, accusing the pontiff of endangering Catholics through his Iran stance, amid Strait of Hormuz ceasefire talks and warnings about Iranian nuclear ambitions, has intensified trader focus on his public rhetoric. Heightened speaking schedule since May 1, including May 6 foreign policy remarks referencing nuclear threats, Project Freedom, and casual topics like economy and culture, underscores his unscripted style driving market sentiment. Upcoming White House briefings, potential summits, and China tariffs addresses through May 31 could prompt further mentions, with resolution based solely on publicly accessible audio or video recordings excluding written posts like Truth Social.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Taiwanese Premier Cho Jung-tai out by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Cho Jung-tai ceases to be Premier of Taiwan for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Cho Jung-tai's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Cho Jung-tai and the government of Taiwan; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Taiwan's Premier Cho Jung-tai retains his position amid an intensifying executive-legislative standoff, with the opposition-controlled Legislative Yuan—holding a slim KMT-TPP majority—passing multiple censure motions against him since January 2026 over budget disputes and constitutional authority. Cho has repeatedly refused to countersign opposition-backed amendments on spending and revenue-sharing, sparking accusations of bypassing the legislature and prompting calls for no-confidence votes, which require a simple majority to pass and force resignation or dissolution. Recent flashpoints include his March 2026 self-funded Japan trip for the World Baseball Classic, criticized by opponents and Beijing, and orders barring information access to certain lawmakers. Traders eye potential no-confidence motions or shifts ahead of November 2026 local elections.

markets.question: Grant vs. Mboko: Match O/U 22.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Tyra Caterina Grant and Victoria Mboko in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Tyra Caterina Grant vs Victoria Mboko Match O/U 22.5

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Tyra Caterina Grant vs Victoria Mboko
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Tyra Caterina Grant and Victoria Mboko in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Tyra Caterina Grant' if Tyra Caterina Grant advances against Victoria Mboko.

This market will resolve to 'Victoria Mboko' if Victoria Mboko advances against Tyra Caterina Grant.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: No. 10 seed Victoria Mboko, ranked No. 9, holds a clear edge as trader consensus favorite over wildcard Tyra Caterina Grant, ranked No. 234, in this Internazionali BNL d'Italia second-round clash on Rome's clay courts. The 19-year-old Canadian brings top-10 power and experience, though she arrives off a recent Madrid setback. Eighteen-year-old Italian Grant, a rising prospect with junior Grand Slam doubles titles, notched her first WTA 1000 main-draw win yesterday, grinding out a three-set victory over compatriot Lisa Pigato amid home-crowd support. No prior head-to-head exists; Grant's 10-3 clay record this year adds upset potential, but Mboko's superior ranking and form drive market sentiment, with weather and fatigue from Grant's marathon opener as key variables.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely contested Democratic primary for New York's 12th Congressional District on June 23—Rep. Jerry Nadler's open seat after his retirement—traders price Assemblymember Alex Bores as the frontrunner at 47.5% implied probability, propelled by over $2.2 million raised in his first quarter and a fresh $3.5 million super PAC boost from a crypto billionaire on May 1, countering heavy attacks from AI industry donors like OpenAI and Palantir executives over his state AI safety bill. Assemblymember Micah Lasher follows at 35%, leveraging endorsements from Nadler, Gov. Kathy Hochul, and Michael Bloomberg, though a recent confrontation with a transgender activist at a Stonewall Democrats meeting has sparked backlash. Jack Schlossberg garners 18% on Kennedy family name recognition and social media buzz, as candidate forums highlight foreign policy divides in this Manhattan battleground.

markets.question: Kawkab AC vs. RCA Zemamra: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Morocco Botola Pro game between Kawkab AC and RCA Zemamra, scheduled for May 8 at 4:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Kawkab AC and RCA Zemamra combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on frmf.ma. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kawkab Marrakech hosts RCA Zemamra in Botola Pro Round 19's return leg on May 8 at Grand Stadium of Marrakech, following their 1-1 first-leg draw in October 2025. Both mid-table sides vie for points in a congested standings battle, with Kawkab around 9th-12th on roughly 15-16 points from 16 games marked by defensive solidity (eight draws) but scant goals, while Zemamra sits nearby after a 1-2 home loss to MAS Fes on April 26 exposed defensive frailties amid mixed recent form. Kawkab's home advantage and head-to-head parity could tilt trader consensus, though no major injuries or suspensions alter lineups; watch pre-match team news for starting XI shifts in this evenly matched fixture.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Mike Minogue's landslide victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26, capturing over 70% of delegate votes for the gubernatorial endorsement, has driven trader consensus to price him at 80.5% to win the September 1 Republican primary. This momentum, combined with recent polls showing a 13-point lead, reflects his appeal as a political newcomer emphasizing fiscal accountability amid voter frustration with rising costs. Brian Shortsleeve qualified for the ballot with 15% support but has seen odds drop to 11.8% amid Minogue's pressure campaign for party unity and refusal to debate. Mike Kennealy's 1.4% reflects failure to clear the convention threshold, narrowing the field despite his prior polling strength. The crowded primary has consolidated behind Minogue as the strongest general election challenger to incumbent Gov. Maura Healey.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Nicolás Maduro at 62.3% to officially lead Venezuela by December 31, 2026, as government directories and institutional listings continue recognizing him as head of state despite his January 3 U.S. military capture under the Trump administration. Delcy Rodríguez, Maduro's former vice president sworn in as acting president by the Supreme Court days later, commands 23.5% amid her recent purges of rivals and efforts to tighten control over Chavista hardliners and intelligence services. U.S. sanction relief in early April underscores fragile transition dynamics, while opposition leaders like María Corina Machado (8.5%) face entrenched barriers. Slow regime change and potential elections keep probabilities fluid.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts modestly toward a Houston high of 80-81°F (27% implied probability) on May 9, with 82-83°F (21%) and 78-79°F (16.5%) close behind, capturing model ensemble spread amid inherent 2-3 day forecast uncertainty. National Weather Service guidance and major models (GFS, ECMWF, Canadian) converge on afternoon highs near 81°F under partly cloudy conditions, moderated by Gulf of Mexico moisture fueling sea breeze circulation and potential cumulus cloud development that caps peak heating. Differentiating factors include divergent simulations of diurnal convection timing, boundary layer winds (northerly shear vs. southerly flow), and residual soil moisture from prior showers limiting sensible heating below climatological May norms of ~86°F. Watch 00Z model runs overnight and NWS updates tomorrow for refinements ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult someone on May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks any non-fictional individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner on the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."

events.title: Internazionali BNL d'Italia: Tallon Griekspoor vs Alexander Blockx
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Tallon Griekspoor and Alexander Blockx in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, originally scheduled for May 8, 2026 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Tallon Griekspoor' if Tallon Griekspoor advances against Alexander Blockx.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Blockx' if Alexander Blockx advances against Tallon Griekspoor.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alexander Blockx's implied 67.5% probability reflects his explosive clay-court form during the spring swing, including a breakthrough semifinal run at the Mutua Madrid Open where the 21-year-old Belgian upset defending champion Casper Ruud and notched three straight Top 20 victories before falling to Alexander Zverev. Now ranked a career-high No. 36, Blockx backed his momentum with a gritty three-set comeback over wildcard Federico Cina in the Internazionali BNL d'Italia first round Thursday. Tallon Griekspoor, seeded No. 29 at No. 31 overall with an 8-10 record this year, enters via a bye after a modest Madrid showing—edging Damir Dzumhur before losing to Lorenzo Musetti—with no head-to-head history favoring the Dutchman's experience on the slower Roman clay.

eventMetadata.context_description: Blake Miguez holds a clear trader consensus edge at 52.5% implied probability in the May 16 Republican primary for Louisiana's open 5th Congressional District seat, driven by his dominant fundraising with $4 million cash on hand reported mid-April, President Trump's early endorsement, and the NRA's sole backing in the race announced April 28. A Rainey Center poll from early April showed a tight contest with Miguez at 23% and Michael Echols at 20% among likely voters, but Miguez's financial and America First credentials have widened his lead despite Echols' local residency advantage and attacks labeling Miguez a carpetbagger from outside the district. Recent independent expenditures include pro-Echols funding from GOPAC and Louisiana Growth And Future Fund in late April, countered by anti-Miguez ads, as early voting continues ahead of election day.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's trader consensus at 70.5% implied probability for the 2026 Roland Garros title stems from his dominant clay-court swing, including Monte-Carlo Masters victory over Carlos Alcaraz on April 12 and a record fifth straight Masters 1000 crown in Madrid on May 3 against Alexander Zverev, extending a 28-match win streak at the level with just two sets dropped in two months. Alcaraz's April 24 withdrawal as two-time defending champion due to a lingering right wrist injury—also sidelining him from Rome—removes the top threat, elevating Sinner as top seed and world No. 1. Zverev (7.2%) draws support from his 2024 final run and consistent clay results, while Djokovic (5.7%) relies on Grand Slam pedigree despite age and recent form, leaving a wide-open field beyond the elite trio.

markets.question: Will Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) hit (LOW) $570 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

However, the stock sold off sharply post-earnings on raised 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion for AI infrastructure, pressuring margins amid competitive ad tech dynamics. Current levels near $617 align below the consensus analyst price target of $840, signaling undervaluation potential; key swing factors include broader market sentiment, Treasury yields, and ad spending trends, with Q2 earnings in late July as the next major catalyst.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects deep skepticism over federal enactment of comprehensive AI safety legislation before 2027, driven by congressional gridlock and the Trump administration's March 2026 National AI Policy Framework advocating light-touch rules that preempt burdensome state laws while prioritizing innovation over stringent safety mandates. Recent bipartisan advances, such as the Senate Judiciary Committee's unanimous April 30 approval of the GUARD Act mandating age verification and banning AI companions for minors, remain narrowly focused on child protection rather than broad risk classification or high-risk AI oversight. No major safety bill has cleared key committees amid competing appropriations battles and the 119th Congress's lame-duck session approaching, with states like Oregon enacting chatbot safeguards on May 1, underscoring the federal void.

markets.question: Will another result occur for the next governing coalition of New Zealand?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.
